questions.question: Who among the following officials was responsible for maintaining law and order in a village during Sher Shah’s administration?
In Brahmadeya villages under the Pallavas, which caste predominantly managed the local Sabha assemblies?
During the period of medieval Indian literature, which language began to decline as the dominant medium for Buddhist literary expression and scholarship?
In which of the following years was Burhan-ul-Mulk appointed the Nazim of Awadh?
Sher Shah divided his empire into shiqqs, equivalent to which administrative division under the Mughals?
Amir Khusrau is known for contributing to which two languages?
Who among the following Delhi sultans accidentally died during a game of polo in the year 1210?
Which among the following South Indian kingdoms is known for the conquest that crossed the river Ganga in the north?
During the Chola rule, which of the following was the land donated to Jaina institutions?
What was the capital of Awadh when it was established in 1722?
In which of the following years did Vasco da Gama land in Calicut on the Malabar Coast for the second time?
Alauddin Khilji introduced the Dagh and Huliyah systems to control the army. What was Huliyah associated with?
Yagnavarman, Sardulavarman and Anantavarman were the kings of which of the following kingdoms?
In the Gupta period, the term Udranga referred to ______.
Who among the following Mughal rulers is considered to be the last powerful Mughal ruler?
During Shahjahan’s rule, how many mansabdars out of 8000 were higher ranking mansabdars who received 61.5% of the total estimated revenue of the empire as salaries for themselves and their troopers?
Who was the first Portuguese governor in India, sent in 1505 to establish naval supremacy?
When was the Gurjara Pratihara dynasty founded by Nagabhatta I in the region of Malwa?
Among which of the following powers did the ‘Tripartite Struggle’ take place during the 8ᵗʰ and 9ᵗʰ centuries?
Which of the following dynasties was NOT involved in the struggle for the control of the prosperous city of Kannauj in northern India?
Rohilla chief, Najib-ud-Daula, played a key role in the ____.
What was the main source of revenue for the Pallava empire, used for maintaining their army and administrative expenses?
Abu’l Fazl was the court historian of which of the following rulers?
The Pallava kings adopted which title to reflect that their rule was guided by the principles of the Dharmashastras?
The five village committees (variyams) such as neernilai variyam and needhi variyam were a unique administrative feature of which of the following dynasties?
